items.name: ErrJobNotFound
items.protection: none needed — assigned once in var block at package init, never written again
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: Declared and initialised at line 9 via var block; Go guarantees package-level var init completes before any goroutine can observe the value, and no subsequent write sites exist in this file.

items.name: ErrJobNotRunning
items.protection: none needed — assigned once in var block at package init, never written again
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: Same reasoning as ErrJobNotFound (line 10); package-level sentinel errors are effectively immutable after init.

items.name: JobStatusUnspecified

items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: Declared as a typed const at line 36; constants cannot be written at runtime so no synchronisation is required.

items.name: JobStatusRunning
items.protection: none needed — Go constants are compile-time immutable

items.name: Job.Info
items.protection: unverified — no lock acquire/release visible in this file
items.comment_claim: Line 14 comment: 'ID and Owner are set once at init; Status and ExitCode are mutated (JM's mutex)'
items.status_reason: The struct field is declared at line 15 but all read/write sites are in other files; whether the JM RWMutex is consistently held around every Info access cannot be confirmed from this file alone.

items.name: Job.Cmd
items.protection: unverified — no lock visible in this file; relies on happens-before from init sequence
items.comment_claim: Lines 16-17 comment: 'set once at init and never written again'
items.status_reason: Declared at line 17; if the write truly occurs before any goroutine that reads it is started, no lock is needed — but that ordering guarantee must be confirmed in the calling code outside this file.

items.name: Job.stopped
items.protection: unverified — no lock acquire/release visible in this file
items.comment_claim: Lines 19-20 comment: 'may be updated after init. uses JobManager's RWMutex mutex'
items.status_reason: Declared at line 20; all actual read/write sites are in other files, so whether the RWMutex is held at every access site cannot be confirmed from this file alone.

items.name: Job.output
items.protection: unverified — no lock acquire/release visible in this file
items.comment_claim: Lines 22-23 comment: 'may be updated after init. uses Cond's mutex'
items.status_reason: Declared at line 23; the pointer itself (not just the buffer contents) is claimed to be protected by a Cond mutex, but no access sites are visible in this file to confirm consistent locking.

items.status_reason: Declared at line 27; treated as immutable after init per comment, but the init-then-goroutine ordering must be verified in the construction and launch code outside this file.

items.status_reason: Declared at line 28; same reasoning as JobInfo.ID — immutability claim cannot be confirmed without seeing the construction site.

items.status_reason: Declared at line 29 and flagged as goroutine_write; all write sites are in other files, so whether the JM mutex is held at every write and every read cannot be confirmed here.

items.name: JobManager.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: JobManager.jobs
items.protection: sync.RWMutex (jm.mu): writes under Lock(), reads under RLock() or Lock()
items.comment_claim: Line 15 comment: 'using mutex to protect the job lifecycles state'
items.status_reason: All four access sites are consistently protected: write at line ~58 (Start) under mu.Lock(); read at line ~73 (Status) under mu.RLock(); read at line ~107 (Stop) under mu.Lock(); read at line ~125 (Output) under mu.RLock(). wait() holds mu.Lock() but only accesses job.Info fields, not the map itself.

items.name: OutputBuffer.mu
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.cond
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism

items.name: OutputBuffer.buf
items.owner: OutputBuffer, shared between writer goroutine and one or more reader goroutines
items.protection: ob.mu held at every access: appended under ob.mu.Lock in Write (line 40-44), read via len and slice in Read (lines 55, 61) under obr.ob.mu.Lock
items.comment_claim: Write docstring says 'wake readers' but makes no explicit claim about which lock protects buf; no other comments claim protection
items.status_reason: Every read and write of ob.buf occurs inside ob.mu.Lock/Unlock with no observed unguarded access site.

items.name: OutputBuffer.done
items.owner: OutputBuffer, written by OutputBuffer.Close and read by reader goroutines
items.protection: ob.mu held at every access: written under ob.mu.Lock in OutputBuffer.Close (line 79), read in Read (lines 55, 65) under obr.ob.mu.Lock (same mutex)
items.comment_claim: OutputBuffer.Close docstring says 'marks buffer done and wakes readers' but does not explicitly name the protecting mutex
items.status_reason: ob.done is set only at line 80 inside ob.mu and read only at lines 55/65 also inside ob.mu; no unprotected access found.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.ob
items.owner: OutputBufferReader, immutable pointer set at construction
items.protection: written once during struct literal initialisation in NewReader (line 30) before the value escapes; never reassigned; all subsequent uses dereference it to acquire ob.mu — the pointer itself is never mutated concurrently
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: obr.ob is assigned exactly once at line 30 (construction) and thereafter treated as a read-only pointer; no concurrent write site exists.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.offset
items.protection: ob.mu held at every access: compared against len(ob.buf) at line 55 and used to slice ob.buf at line 61 and incremented at line 62, all inside obr.ob.mu.Lock in Read
items.comment_claim: none
items.status_reason: obr.offset is read at lines 55 and 61 and written at line 62, all within the ob.mu critical section acquired at line 49; no access outside that lock.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.closed
items.owner: OutputBufferReader, written by OutputBufferReader.Close and read by Read
items.protection: ob.mu held at every access: written under obr.ob.mu.Lock in OutputBufferReader.Close (line 89), read in the Wait-loop condition at line 55 under obr.ob.mu.Lock in Read
items.comment_claim: OutputBufferReader.Close docstring says 'unblocks any pending Read' implying the flag and Broadcast are the coordination mechanism, but does not name the protecting mutex explicitly
items.status_reason: obr.closed is written at line 90 inside ob.mu and read at line 55 also inside ob.mu; both goroutines share the same mutex, so no unprotected access exists.

items.name: OutputBufferReader.drained
items.status_reason: sync primitive — is its own protection mechanism
